claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 2 children:
  - Alex is 12 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 8 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 12 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me first review the eligibility rules so I can ask you the right questions.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for your patience. To help determine your eligibility for Child Benefit, I need to ask you some questions.

First, do you have any children, and if so, how many?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I'd like to know if I'm eligible for Child Benefit. I have two children - Alex, who's 12, and Blake, who's 8. They both live with me here at home.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that information! It's helpful to know you have two children - Alex (12) and Blake (8) - who both live with you at home.

To assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex and Blake, I need to ask a few more questions.

Do you live in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - we're in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's great, thank you. So you live in the UK with Alex and Blake in Manchester.

Are either Alex or Blake currently in hospital, in care, or in any residential accommodation?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, neither of them are in hospital or care or anything like that. They're both just at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent, thank you for confirming that. 

Just to make sure I have all the information I need: Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for either Alex or Blake?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for them. It's just me looking after them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you for that information. Just one more question to complete my assessment:

Are Alex and Blake both in education (attending school)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, they're both in school - just regular primary and secondary school.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. Let me now assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex and Blake.

Based on what you've told me:
- You have two children: Alex (12) and Blake (8)
- Both children live with you in the UK (Manchester)
- You are responsible for them
- Neither child is in hospital, care, or residential accommodation
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for either child
- Both children are in education

According to the UK government rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 and you live in the UK. You're usually considered responsible for a child if you live with them.

Since both Alex (12) and Blake (8) are under 16, they both live with you, you're responsible for them, you live in the UK, and no one else is claiming for them, you meet all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit for both children.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible for Child Benefit for both Alex and Blake.**

You meet all the requirements because:
- Both children are under 16 years old
- You live with them in the UK
- You are responsible for them
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for them

You can proceed with your Child Benefit application or claim. Please note that only one person can claim Child Benefit for each child, and once you claim, you'll receive payments for both Alex and Blake.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex is 12 years old (under 16), lives with the claimant in the UK, the claimant is responsible for Alex, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. Alex is in school. According to UK government rules, the claimant qualifies for Child Benefit as they are responsible for a child under 16 and live in the UK. Alex is not in hospital or care for extended periods that would affect eligibility.

Blake is 8 years old (under 16), lives with the claimant in the UK, the claimant is responsible for Blake, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for Blake. Blake is in school. According to UK government rules, the claimant qualifies for Child Benefit as they are responsible for a child under 16 and live in the UK. Blake is not in hospital or care for extended periods that would affect eligibility.
